question.text: Under the SEBI (FPI) Regulations, 2019, the contribution of a single NRI or OCI or resident Indian individual in the corpus of an FPI applicant should be:

answer.text: Below 25 percent of the total contribution
Below 50 percent of the total contribution
Not more than 20 percent of the total contribution
Below 10 percent of the total contribution

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per Regulation 4(c)(i), the contribution of a single NRI or OCI or RI individual should be below 25 percent of the total contribution in the corpus of the applicant. **The Trap:** The 50% threshold is tempting because that is the aggregate limit for all NRIs, OCIs, and RI individuals combined, not the individual limit. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-B (Mutual Fund Foundation) covers NRI investment restrictions in mutual funds which have different thresholds. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember: 25% is the single investor limit, 50% is the aggregate limit for all NRIs/OCIs/RIs combined.
